source.author: SSA Catalog
source.notes: Lamb shawarma marinade with warm spices, garlic, lemon, and herbs—balanced for leg, shoulder, or chops.

ingredients.label: Onion, finely grated
notes: For a smoother marinade, use onion juice only (strain the pulp).

ingredients.label: Plain Greek yogurt
notes: Optional but recommended: helps tenderize and cling to lamb.

ingredients.label: Ground allspice
notes: About 1/2 teaspoon; classic lamb shawarma note.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: About 1 3/4 teaspoons. Sized for ~1.3–1.8 kg (3–4 lb) lamb.
Reduce to 7 g if you will salt again before cooking.

ingredients.label: Dried oregano
notes: About 1 teaspoon; optional, adds herbal backbone.

ingredients.label: Dried mint
notes: About 1/2 teaspoon; optional but pairs beautifully with lamb.
Sub: 1 tbsp fresh mint, finely chopped.

ingredients.label: Honey
notes: Optional: balances acidity and improves browning (about 1 teaspoon).

steps.title: Make the marinade
instructions: In a bowl, whisk olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, grated garlic, and grated onion until combined.
Whisk in Greek yogurt (if using) until smooth.
Add spices (cumin, coriander, paprika, allspice, cinnamon, cloves, optional cardamom), pepper, salt, optional dried herbs, optional chili flakes, and optional honey. Whisk until evenly blended.

steps.title: Marinate the lamb
instructions: Add lamb (shoulder/leg slices, cubes for skewers, chops, or thin strips) to a zip bag or covered container.
Pour marinade over lamb and massage to coat thoroughly.

steps.title: Marinate time
instructions: Best flavor: 8–24 hours (lamb benefits from longer spice contact).
If using yogurt, keep to 24 hours max for best texture.

steps.title: Cooking guidance (notes)
instructions: For shawarma-style serving, slice lamb thinly across the grain after cooking (or slice thinly before marinating for very fast cooking).
Cook hot for browning: grill, cast-iron skillet, or high-heat oven/roast.
Pat off excess wet marinade if needed to improve searing.

steps.title: Food safety
instructions: Discard used marinade that touched raw meat, or boil thoroughly before using for basting.
